content.text: I found the watcher finalizer closes the libuv handle when the JS object is GC’d; I’ll inspect similar long-lived handle implementations to mirror their lifetime management.

{
  "name": "txiki.js",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "Utilities for txiki.js",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "postinstall": "npm install --prefix website",
    "docs": "npm run build --prefix website",
    "docs:watch": "npm run start --prefix website",
    "docs:deploy": "npm run docs && gh-pages -d website/build -u \"github-actions-bot <support+actions@github.com>\"",
    "lint": "eslint --max-warnings 0 ."
  },
  "license": "MIT",
  "dependencies": {
    "@eslint/js": "^9.39.3",

  compile infile [outfile]

  app <subcommand>

input.description: Show final diff and working tree status

content.text:
Implemented the FileWatcher GC lifetime fix in `src/mod_fswatch.c`.

What changed:
- `TJSFsWatch` now stores a duplicated JS object reference (`fw->obj`) to keep the watcher alive while active.
- The finalizer no longer closes the underlying libuv watcher just because the JS object becomes otherwise unreachable.
- `FileWatcher.close()` now releases the self-reference and closes the libuv handle, allowing cleanup only after explicit close.

Validation performed:
- Wrote a temporary regression test that creates an unreferenced `tjs.watch(...)`, writes to the watched file, and asserts the callback still fires.
- Confirmed the test failed before the fix.
- Rebuilt successfully with:
  - `make -C /testbed`
- Confirmed the regression test passes after the fix.
- Ran the existing FileWatcher test:
  - `/testbed/build/tjs run /testbed/tests/test-fswatch.js`
- Deleted the temporary regression test afterward.

Note:
- I did not commit anything.
- Final working tree still shows unrelated pre-existing changes:
  - deleted `tests/test-lookup.js`
  - deleted `tests/test-stdio-stream-reuse.js`
  - untracked `reports/`

result:
Implemented the FileWatcher GC lifetime fix in `src/mod_fswatch.c`.

What changed:
- `TJSFsWatch` now stores a duplicated JS object reference (`fw->obj`) to keep the watcher alive while active.
- The finalizer no longer closes the underlying libuv watcher just because the JS object becomes otherwise unreachable.
- `FileWatcher.close()` now releases the self-reference and closes the libuv handle, allowing cleanup only after explicit close.

Validation performed:
- Wrote a temporary regression test that creates an unreferenced `tjs.watch(...)`, writes to the watched file, and asserts the callback still fires.
- Confirmed the test failed before the fix.
- Rebuilt successfully with:
  - `make -C /testbed`
- Confirmed the regression test passes after the fix.
- Ran the existing FileWatcher test:
  - `/testbed/build/tjs run /testbed/tests/test-fswatch.js`
- Deleted the temporary regression test afterward.

Note:
- I did not commit anything.
- Final working tree still shows unrelated pre-existing changes:
  - deleted `tests/test-lookup.js`
  - deleted `tests/test-stdio-stream-reuse.js`
  - untracked `reports/`

content.text: Acknowledged. That background regression-test process was one of the intentionally stopped hanging runs, so exit code `143` is expected from termination. The later updated regression test with explicit `tjs.exit(0)` completed successfully, and the temporary test file was removed.

content.text: Acknowledged. That was also an earlier hanging background test process that I stopped, so exit code `143` is expected from termination. The final version of the temporary regression test was run afterward in the foreground and completed successfully with no output.
